explanation: Asiatic acid is a triterpenoid compound from Centella asiatica (gotu kola) that directly stimulates collagen synthesis and supports wound healing and barrier repair. It is one of the key actives responsible for centella's well-documented skin-regenerating properties and is non-irritating and well-tolerated across skin types.

Asiatic pennywort extract is a common name for Centella asiatica extract, containing triterpenoids (asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid) with proven anti-inflammatory, wound-healing, and collagen-stimulating properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and widely used in K-beauty and sensitive-skin formulas.

Asiaticoside is a triterpenoid glycoside from Centella asiatica that stimulates collagen synthesis and promotes skin repair by triggering fibroblast proliferation. It is the primary active in centella-based formulations and is non-irritating and well-tolerated by all skin types including reactive and sensitized.

Aspalathus linearis (rooibos) leaf extract is a South African red bush botanical rich in aspalathin and quercetin with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated, caffeine-free, and used in protective and soothing formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and calm reactive skin.

Astaxanthin is a carotenoid pigment synthesized by microalgae and regarded as one of the most potent natural antioxidants, with singlet oxygen quenching activity many times greater than vitamin E. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas to defend against UV-induced oxidative damage and support skin elasticity.

Avena sativa (oat) kernel extract is rich in beta-glucan and avenanthramides, clinically documented to reduce histamine-triggered itch, calm redness, and reinforce barrier function. It is among the most well-tolerated botanical extracts for reactive and eczema-prone skin.

Avena sativa (oat) kernel flour is finely milled oat flour used in skincare as a gentle physical exfoliant and soothing agent, delivering avenanthramides and beta-glucans that calm irritation and reduce itch. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and commonly used in cleansing and mask formulas for sensitive and reactive skin.

Avena sativa (oat) kernel oil is rich in linoleic acid, oleic acid, and vitamin E, supporting barrier repair and providing lightweight emollient conditioning. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and has low comedogenic potential, making it suitable for dry-to-combination skin types.

Avena sativa (oat) seed water is a water-phase oat extraction delivering soluble beta-glucans and avenanthramides with soothing and anti-inflammatory benefits. It is well-tolerated and used as a gentle hydrating base in toners and mists for reactive and sensitized skin.

Avena sativa kernel extract is an alternate INCI listing for oat kernel extract containing beta-glucan and avenanthramides with clinically documented soothing, anti-itch, and barrier-supporting properties. It is among the most well-tolerated botanical actives for sensitive and reactive skin types.

Avena sativa kernel flour is an alternate INCI listing for finely milled oat flour, used as a gentle exfoliant and soothing agent delivering avenanthramides that calm itch and reduce redness. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and widely used in formulas for reactive and eczema-prone skin.

Avena sativa kernel oil is an alternate INCI listing for oat kernel oil, a lightweight emollient rich in linoleic acid and vitamin E that supports skin barrier integrity. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and has low comedogenic potential.

Avena sativa meal extract is derived from whole oat meal and delivers soluble beta-glucans and avenanthramide polyphenols with soothing and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in calming formulas for reactive, compromised, or eczema-prone skin.

Avocado oil & green flora complex is a proprietary blend name rather than a standard INCI ingredient — likely a combination of avocado oil (Persea gratissima oil) with one or more botanical extracts. The avocado oil component is a rich emollient high in oleic acid and vitamins D and E, well-suited for dry and mature skin; the specific content of the 'green flora complex' varies by manufacturer and cannot be fully assessed without the complete formulation.

Azelaic acid is a naturally occurring dicarboxylic acid found in wheat and barley with well-documented anti-inflammatory, comedolytic, and melanin-inhibiting properties, used to treat acne, rosacea, and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation. At concentrations of 10–20% it is effective and generally well-tolerated, though it can cause mild tingling on first application on reactive or barrier-compromised skin.

Azeleic acid is an alternate or misspelled listing of azelaic acid — a naturally occurring dicarboxylic acid with anti-inflammatory, comedolytic, and melanin-inhibiting properties used to treat acne, rosacea, and hyperpigmentation. At effective concentrations of 10–20% it is generally well-tolerated with occasional mild tingling on first use.

Bakuchiol is a meroterpene phenol from Psoralea corylifolia seeds that activates retinoid-like signaling pathways to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve fine lines and skin texture without the irritation associated with retinol. It is photostable, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated by all skin types including reactive and sensitive, making it an effective retinol alternative in gentler routines.

Bambusa vulgaris (bamboo) extract is rich in silica, flavonoids, and phenolic acids with antioxidant and mild astringent properties. It is well-tolerated and used in soothing, firming, and protective formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and contribute to skin texture refinement.

Bambusa vulgaris leaf/stem extract delivers silica and polyphenol antioxidants from bamboo leaves and stems with soothing and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in calming and protective formulas across skin types.

Beeswax is a natural wax secreted by honeybees used in skincare as an occlusive emollient, thickener, and structuring agent in balms, lip products, and creams. It is generally well-tolerated, though its moderate occlusive potential may not suit acne-prone skin at high concentrations; those with bee-product sensitivities should patch-test.

Behentrimonium methosulfate is a mild quaternary ammonium conditioning agent derived from rapeseed oil, widely used in hair conditioners and rinse-off formulas to improve slip and reduce static. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, making it a gentler alternative to more strongly cationic conditioning agents.

Behenyl alcohol is a long-chain C22 fatty alcohol derived from plant or synthetic sources, used as an emollient, emulsifier, and thickener that gives creams a smooth, rich texture. Unlike short-chain alcohols, fatty alcohols do not disrupt the skin barrier — they are well-tolerated and non-irritating across skin types including sensitive.

Bentonite is a natural smectite clay with high oil-absorbing and pore-cleansing capacity, widely used in face masks and cleansers to draw out sebum and impurities. It is well-tolerated in rinse-off formulas, though prolonged leave-on use can over-dry reactive or barrier-compromised skin.

Benzoic acid is a mild organic acid preservative found naturally in berries and resins, used in skincare at low concentrations to inhibit mold and yeast growth. It is generally well-tolerated at typical formula concentrations (0.1–0.5%), though very high concentrations can cause mild irritation on reactive skin.

Benzophenone-3 (oxybenzone) is a chemical UV filter that absorbs UVA and UVB radiation but is also a documented contact and photocontact allergen, and has raised concerns about systemic absorption and potential hormonal disruption. It frequently triggers allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin and is best avoided in leave-on formulas for reactive skin — mineral UV filters are a safer alternative.

Benzophenone-4 is a water-soluble UV filter and UV stabilizer used at low concentrations in sunscreens and photosensitive formulas to protect other ingredients from UV degradation. It is less sensitizing than benzophenone-3 and considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels, though it belongs to the same chemical class and should be used with caution by those with documented benzophenone sensitivity.

Benzoyl peroxide is a potent antimicrobial and keratolytic acne treatment that generates free radicals to kill Cutibacterium acnes bacteria and accelerate surface cell turnover, making it one of the most effective OTC acne actives available. It commonly causes significant dryness, flaking, redness, and irritation — particularly at concentrations of 5–10% — and can bleach fabrics on contact; reactive or sensitive skin is best served by starting at 2.5% with careful barrier support.

Benzyl alcohol is a naturally occurring aromatic alcohol used in skincare as both a fragrance component and a preservative, but classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle in fragrance-sensitive individuals, particularly in leave-on formulas at higher concentrations.

Benzyl benzoate is a fragrance compound and insect-repellent ester found naturally in Balsam of Peru and other botanicals, classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Benzyl glycol is a fragrance solvent and mild preservative booster used to dissolve and stabilize fragrance compounds in skincare formulas, with some antimicrobial activity. It is considered well-tolerated compared to many fragrance-related ingredients and does not carry the same allergen designation as benzyl alcohol or benzyl benzoate.
